articles.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5 with Native Multimodal Reasoning and Extended Context
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5, featuring deeply integrated multimodal reasoning across text, image, audio, and video inputs with a context window exceeding 1 million tokens. The model demonstrates significant improvements in long-horizon planning tasks and multi-step mathematical proofs, narrowing the gap with domain-specific systems. Enterprise API pricing reflects a shift toward consumption-based tiers aimed at making advanced capabilities accessible at scale.

articles.title: Google DeepMind's Gemini Ultra 2 Achieves State-of-the-Art on Agentic Benchmarks
articles.summary: DeepMind announced Gemini Ultra 2, which sets new records on agentic task-completion benchmarks including SWE-bench and WebArena, successfully completing multi-step browser and coding workflows with over 72% accuracy. The model integrates a learned tool-use layer that dynamically selects and chains APIs without explicit prompting. Researchers highlighted a new self-verification loop that substantially reduces hallucinated actions in autonomous workflows.

articles.title: Figure Robotics Begins Commercial Deployment of Humanoid Workers at BMW Plants
articles.summary: Figure AI has started deploying its Figure 03 humanoid robots on BMW assembly lines in South Carolina, marking one of the first scaled commercial deployments of general-purpose humanoid robots in automotive manufacturing. The robots handle bin-picking, part insertion, and quality inspection tasks alongside human workers. Figure reports the units operate autonomously for 18-hour shifts with a mean-time-between-intervention exceeding 4 hours, a milestone for humanoid reliability.

articles.title: Boston Dynamics Atlas Electric Demonstrates Fully Autonomous Warehouse Navigation
articles.summary: Boston Dynamics released footage and technical details of its electric Atlas robot autonomously navigating a complex, unstructured warehouse environment including stairs, narrow aisles, and dynamic obstacles. The system uses a vision-language-action model trained end-to-end on real-world data, eliminating the need for pre-mapped environments. The company announced partnerships with DHL and Amazon to pilot Atlas in logistics facilities by Q3 2026.

articles.title: Anthropic Introduces Constitutional Classifiers to Defend Against Jailbreaks at Scale
articles.summary: Anthropic published a paper and deployed a new safety architecture called Constitutional Classifiers, lightweight auxiliary models trained via constitutional AI principles that screen both inputs and outputs in real time. In red-team evaluations, the system reduced successful jailbreak rates to under 3% while adding less than 40ms of latency. The approach is model-agnostic and Anthropic has open-sourced the training framework to encourage industry-wide adoption.

articles.title: TSMC Begins Volume Production of 1.4nm A14 Process Node Ahead of Schedule
articles.summary: TSMC announced it has commenced high-volume manufacturing on its A14 (1.4nm-class) process node at its Kaohsiung fab, roughly one quarter ahead of its original roadmap. The node uses gate-all-around nanosheet transistors and backside power delivery, achieving an estimated 15% speed improvement and 30% power reduction over N2. Apple, Nvidia, and AMD are confirmed first customers, with chips expected in consumer products by early 2027.

articles.title: Microsoft and Quantinuum Demonstrate Reliable Logical Qubit Operations Below Error Threshold
articles.summary: Microsoft and Quantinuum published results showing sustained logical qubit error rates below 10⁻⁶ across 100+ consecutive operations on their trapped-ion system, crossing a key threshold for practical fault-tolerant quantum computing. The achievement was enabled by Microsoft's new real-time decoding ASIC that performs error correction in under one microsecond. The teams outlined a roadmap to a 100-logical-qubit system by 2028 targeting materials science and cryptographic applications.

articles.title: Neuralink Receives FDA Clearance for Second-Generation Brain-Computer Interface Trial
articles.summary: The FDA has cleared Neuralink to begin human trials of its N2 implant, which doubles the electrode count to 2,048 channels and features an upgraded wireless chipset with 10x higher data throughput than the first-generation device. The approval covers a 30-patient expansion trial focused on enabling full cursor control and text input for individuals with quadriplegia. Preliminary data from the initial cohort showed stable signal quality beyond 12 months post-implantation.
